mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate that has stopped rising after a multi-year hiking cycle signals reduced discount-rate pressure on equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as markets price in a policy pause.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.27% after steady hikes through 2018
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level/trend and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following a flattening FEDFUNDS trend are not significantly higher than baseline (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window from cyclical lows, it signals an inflection toward labor-market deterioration that precedes broader earnings weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9%, near cycle lows as of Dec 2018
failure_condition: Following trigger activation, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous federal funds rate pause combined with a rising unemployment rate signals that the Fed is behind the curve on a genuine labor-market slowdown rather than proactively easing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days because the joint signal indicates recession risk rather than accommodative support.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS flat or falling AND UNRATE rising over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances produce positive median 20d forward returns, or the interaction term's incremental Rank IC over either single-series proxy is not statistically significant (p>0.10) across walk-forward folds.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a deceleration in year-over-year industrial production growth signals fading manufacturing momentum and impending margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; this relationship is expected to be weak or absent during early/mid-cycle regimes when INDPRO deceleration reflects mere normalization rather than contraction risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY growth decelerates for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 109.39 as of Nov 2018, near cycle highs
failure_condition: During late-cycle periods, INDPRO deceleration signals are followed by positive 60d forward returns more than 55% of the time, or Rank IC < 0.03 conditioned on late-cycle regime flag.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects resilient consumer demand that supports near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as a coincident confidence signal.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 513531 million as of Nov 2018
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and forward 5d returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or the sign hit-rate does not exceed 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts exceeding 10% from their trailing 6-month average signals contraction in interest-rate-sensitive construction activity, foreshadowing broader demand weakness and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1256 thousand units as of Nov 2018, below earlier 2018 peaks
failure_condition: Triggered instances show positive median 20d forward returns, or the effect disappears (Rank IC < 0.03) once conditioned outside tightening-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with accelerating CPI inflation signals an overheating labor market that raises the probability of continued monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average gain > 200k AND CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 150263k, CPIAUCSL at 252.733 as of late 2018
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances yield positive median 60d forward returns, or the interaction effect's Rank IC is not significantly greater than the max of the two single-series Rank ICs (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of moderate, stable inflation (CPI YoY between 1.5% and 2.5%), a paused or gently rising federal funds rate is interpreted by markets as balanced policy, supporting short-horizon risk appetite and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days; outside this inflation band, the same FEDFUNDS behavior is not expected to carry positive signal because inflation risk dominates the reaction function.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL YoY near 1.9%, FEDFUNDS 2.27% as of Dec 2018
failure_condition: Within the specified CPI band, 5d forward returns following stable/rising FEDFUNDS are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05), or the effect reverses sign when CPI YoY is outside 1.5-2.5% but the model still predicts positive.
